purpose: Preserve broad conversation context, user corrections, high-value Codex outputs, and next-work gates so future Codex sessions do not lose the big picture.

storage_policy.markdown_policy: Do not save Markdown unless explicitly asked, except required session handoffs under project AGENTS rules.
forbidden: Do not use /Users/joshuaeisenhart/Desktop/Codex Ratchet/system_v4/a2_state as Codex general memory.
Do not write Codex memory into Claude or Hermes private memory directories unless explicitly authorized.
Do not silently canonize provisional scaffolds.

runtime_separation.codex: Owns /Users/joshuaeisenhart/wiki/codex-memory as its wiki memory surface.
runtime_separation.a2_state: Project/runtime state for A2, not Codex private external memory.
runtime_separation.claude: Separate runtime with separate memory surfaces.
runtime_separation.hermes: Separate runtime with separate memory surfaces.
runtime_separation.wiki: Shared reservoir; runtime-specific directories remain single-writer unless explicitly imported.

user_said_to_preserve.content: Codex had a lot of good context and things the user said in the thread that should be saved. The 55-item list was an example of something Codex should never have forgotten.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Preserve broad context packets, not only final artifacts or tiny summaries.

user_said_to_preserve.content: Do not make A2 state Codex's memory state.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: A2 state is never the target for general Codex memory packets.

user_said_to_preserve.content: Make a Codex-owned system in the wiki; Codex should have its own directory rather than writing into Claude/Hermes directories or A2 state.
The 55-item list was made by Codex and is not canon. It can be improved; additional people, schools, and traditions may be added.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Mark the list as provisional scaffold with admission gates.

user_said_to_preserve.content: Council is not everything. It is a voice or audit layer for pushback, divergence maintenance, and auditing. It does not have to use the voices, but can.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Do not collapse council into controller, worker pool, tools, or the main project.

user_said_to_preserve.content: Voices are not the council. Council can run as a voice/check/audit layer, but voices and lanes should remain distinct.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Keep voice topology and council auditing separate in prompts and receipts.

user_said_to_preserve.content: Do not over-fixate on a cap like six agents. Codex can run up to the configured runtime cap and then run rolling waves.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Track spawned, blocked, and deferred lanes. Close stale workers and continue in waves.

user_said_to_preserve.content: The user wants actual wave runs and receipts, not local commentary claiming the council or agents ran.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Visible plurality must be receipted. Do not fake worker execution.

user_said_to_preserve.content: User feedback is not itself the whole prompt. Use the feedback to tune the prompt and then do the actual work.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Capture corrections, then route into concrete MMM, tooling, or sim tasks.

user_said_to_preserve.content: The system had lost strategy and the big picture. The user wants the larger project stabilized and then a return to sims.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Every MMM/council step needs a return-to-sims gate.

user_said_to_preserve.content: MMMs are made to stabilize reasoning and prevent collapse; once stabilized, go straight back to sims.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Do not let MMM tuning become a permanent side project.

user_said_to_preserve.content: Mach, Faraday, and later Wittgenstein were mentioned relative to the massive list, not as the whole list or center.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Treat named thinkers as entries or candidates inside a broader source-field manifold.

user_said_to_preserve.content: Hume matters as a serious empiricist/nominalist stance. Kant is constrained or contrastive, and should not become a hidden organizing center.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Preserve Humean evidence-scope and nominalist caution; gate Kantian/rationalist moves through evidence and operational checks.

user_said_to_preserve.content: The user wants other tools integrated, including Z3 and proof/formal tools, to keep things real.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Future waves should include formal-methods/proof-tool lanes when claims can be made checkable.

user_said_to_preserve.content: tmux or OMX control had been getting lost and should remain part of the orchestration picture.
user_said_to_preserve.operational_effect: Keep terminal/session orchestration as a real systems lane, not an afterthought.

codex_outputs_to_preserve.name: MMM 55-item source-field scaffold
codex_outputs_to_preserve.why_preserve: It provides a broad source-field spine for mini-MMM language bodies and should not be forgotten, but must remain improvable.

codex_outputs_to_preserve.name: Codex wiki memory manifest
codex_outputs_to_preserve.why_preserve: It separates Codex memory from A2 state and other runtime directories.

codex_outputs_to_preserve.name: Work handoff for context capture
codex_outputs_to_preserve.why_preserve: It tracks the decision chain and prevents the memory correction from being lost again.

operating_rules_to_reuse: Preserve user ideas separately from Codex-generated scaffolds.
Preserve good LLM content with provenance, status, and admission gates.
Treat source-field lists as mutable scaffolds unless explicitly admitted as canon.
Keep council, voices, workers, tools, and controller roles separate.
Use subagent waves for plurality-sensitive work, with spawned/blocked/deferred truth.
Run audits for missing receipts, fake plurality, status inflation, and local-summary collapse.
Use proof/formal tools when claims can be constrained into checkable form.
Return to sims once MMMs are stable enough to improve sim work.

whole_context_voices: Systems: loops, incentives, couplings, second-order effects.
Factory: bottlenecks, queue shape, handoff boundaries, leverage.
Strategy: whole-project why, sequence, drift, retreat condition.
prompt_specific_voices: Popper: claim, falsifier, decisive check, killed/open/survived.
Zhuangzi: multiple live readings kept distinct until exclusion is earned.
voice_and_council_memory.bridge_voice: Hume: clear, warm, common-sense executive summary; humane, evidence-scoped, nominalist, testable.
voice_and_council_memory.council: Lightweight pushback/divergence/audit layer; may invoke voices but is not identical to them.

policy: Use rolling waves rather than treating one cap as a strategic limit.
Close stale completed workers before claiming capacity.
Visible lanes require receipts or must be marked blocked/deferred.
Do not silently absorb overflow into controller-local synthesis.

project_big_picture.why_mmm: Mini-MMM language bodies shape worker salience and preserve multiple live methods so subagents produce useful, divergent, evidence-bounded outputs.
project_big_picture.why_memory: Without durable memory, Codex forgets the user's corrections and reverts to local summaries, fake convergence, or over-tuned council mechanics.
project_big_picture.why_sims_next: The actual project value is in returning to sims with better admissibility, blocker clarity, tool integration, and checkable experiments.
project_big_picture.drift_signature: More prompt-law tuning, voice polish, or council mechanics without better sim questions, proof checks, or concrete queue movement.
project_big_picture.retreat_condition: If MMM/council work stops improving sim readiness, stop expanding it and run sim-side tasks.

candidate_improvements_to_source_field: Add candidate people/schools only when they change worker behavior or sim routing.
Do not center a thinker merely because they are famous or recently mentioned.
Prefer entries that produce operational distinctions, measurement gates, falsifiers, or better queues.
Constrain rationalist, essentialist, mystical, and authority-first sources unless translated into testable operations.

next_actions.action: Validate this JSON packet and copy it into wiki Codex memory.
next_actions.done_when: python3 -m json.tool succeeds on the copied file.

next_actions.action: Update MANIFEST.json to reference this broader packet.
next_actions.done_when: Manifest current_packets contains both context packets and validates as JSON.

next_actions.action: Run or record worker receipts for the memory-capture wave.
next_actions.done_when: Receipts are summarized without pretending unreturned workers completed.

next_actions.action: Return to MMM dictionary or sim-readiness work.
next_actions.done_when: Next task produces a concrete dictionary kernel, sim question, proof-tool check, or queue move.

open_questions: Which thread fragments should be promoted from context memory into doctrine candidates?
Should Codex build an index file over context packets for automatic retrieval?
Should Claude and Hermes receive separate import packets, or only read the wiki Codex memory packet by reference?
Which proof/Z3 lane should be the first real tool-integration wave?
